strengths: Best visual fidelity: vehicles have windows (rgba cyan), wheels (dark circles), headlights (yellow rects, direction-aware), lane markings (dashed lines), log wood grain + end caps, turtle shells as ellipses with smaller inner ellipse pattern + head
Complete 5-sound audio: hop 600Hz square 30ms, splash noise burst 150ms, squash 110Hz 100ms, bay fill 440→880Hz ascending, level complete 5-note arpeggio (300→800Hz)
All 4 game states (IDLE/PLAYING/PAUSED/GAME_OVER) with correct Enter/P transitions
Frog drawn as roundRect with circular eyes + white pupils + leg rectangles — closely matches spec's 'rounded square with eyes' description
Dive cycle: 7s total (3s visible, 1s sinking, 2s submerged, 1s rising) with 8Hz blink via Math.floor(t*8)%2 — matches spec exactly
River riding: platform velocity added to frog position each frame; off-screen check (x < -20 or x > W+20) kills frog
Forward scoring: maxRow tracked, +10 only for net progress — correct per spec
Timer bar at y=650: green→yellow→red gradient, 580px max width, shrinking correctly
Home bays at correct x positions [20,140,260,380,500] with 48×36px, filled bays show mini frog icon with dark green body + white eyes
Death animation: frog flashes red (squash) or expanding ripple circles (splash) for 600ms
divePhase per-platform offset (i * 1.5) creates staggered diving between turtle groups
Level progression: speedMul *= 1.15 per level — correct 15% increase
localStorage high score with 'frogger_hs' key
weaknesses: Hop animation interpolation uses `frog.x + (frog.tx - frog.x) * (t * 1.1)` which overshoots at t>0.91 then snaps back — visually jittery hop
River riding during hop: code returns early during hop ('don't do river riding during hop') which means frog briefly doesn't move with platform during 80ms hop, causing micro-drift
Vehicle makePlatforms uses simple while-loop x distribution which can create uneven spacing near screen edges

Uses event.code for input BUT the keydown handler checks `!prev` (first-press only) inside the handler — this means keyup/re-press is needed for each hop, but combined with hopAnim debounce this is correct behavior

Turtle dive grouping: divePhase is per-platform (i * 1.5 offset), not per-group. Spec says 'every 3rd group' and 'every 2nd group', but all turtles in a lane have individual offsets creating a wave pattern rather than grouped diving

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable and polished Frogger with solid movement, hazards, bays, timer, lives, pause and restart, but visuals are only an approximation of the arcade original and there are minor logic/presentation quirks such as timer death using squash and update occurring after draw causing a one-frame visual lag.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core Frogger mechanics are implemented with road traffic, river logs/turtles with diving, home bays, timer, and lives, but the hop animation has a lerp bug (uses current position in interpolation formula causing non-linear jumps), and draw happens before update each frame causing a one-frame visual lag.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented Frogger clone with smooth animations, accurate collision logic, and a complete game loop including audio synthesis.

strengths: Most correct river riding implementation: ridingVx set per-frame from platform speed, applied both during hop animation AND idle — frog tracks platform seamlessly even while hopping
Cleanest grid-based movement: frog.col/frog.row track grid position; visual position interpolated from grid coords during hop, snapped after. Most faithful to spec's 'grid-snapped hops'
justPressed{} pattern for input: keys buffered on keydown, consumed after update — prevents double-hops and correctly debounces
event.code for ALL input (ArrowUp/KeyW/ArrowDown/KeyS/ArrowLeft/KeyA/ArrowRight/KeyD/Enter/KeyP) — layout-independent per spec
Dive cycle exactly per spec: 7s (3s visible, 1s sinking blink, 2s submerged, 1s rising blink). Blink uses Math.floor(divePhase*8)%2 — 8Hz
diveEvery property per lane: lane 7 diveEvery=3, lane 9 diveEvery=2 — correct 'every 3rd group' / 'every 2nd group' from spec
Forward scoring with maxRowReached track — only awards +10 for new forward progress
Home bays with mini frog icon via drawFrog() reuse at 20px scale — nice visual consistency
Level progression: speeds multiply by pow(1.15, level-1) at lane creation — correct per spec
Timer bar at y=H-12: 580px, green→yellow→red based on fraction thresholds (>0.5/0.25)
5 complete audio effects: hop, splash, squash, fillBay (440→880Hz ascending), levelComplete (4-note arpeggio 440→554→659→880Hz)
Clean separation: createVehicles/createPlatforms recreated per level with updated speed multiplier
Death animation: 0.5s flash at 6Hz (Math.floor(deathTimer*6)%2)
Start screen with background zones visible behind semi-transparent overlay + large frog icon
Vehicle visual detail: trucks have dark cab sections, cars have rounded rect bodies + windshields
Log visual: rounded rect with stroke grain lines and shell pattern on turtles (nested ellipses)
weaknesses: Turtle blink on sinking/rising: `if (Math.floor(p.divePhase * 8) % 2 === 0) continue` — this skips drawing when even, but divePhase is 3.0–4.0 or 6.0–7.0, so the blink frequency depends on absolute divePhase value rather than relative time within the sinking/rising window. Still produces visible blinking but at irregular perceived rate
Vehicle collision uses row matching (Math.abs(lane.y - fy) > 5) which could miss collisions if frog is between visual rows during ride-carry
Lives display shows `lives - 1` frog icons (spare lives only) — spec doesn't specify whether current life counts, but this matches arcade convention

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable Frogger with solid movement, hazards, homes, lives, timer, pause and restart, but visual authenticity is only moderate and there are some logic/polish issues around platform carrying and home-bay/layout fidelity.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Solid Frogger implementation with road traffic, river platforms with diving turtles, home bays, scoring, lives, timer, level progression, and sound effects; minor issues include hop animation being very fast (0.08s) and platform collision using frog center which can feel slightly off, but overall well-executed.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented Frogger clone with smooth animations, working turtle diving mechanics, and a complete game loop including audio.

strengths: 4 game states (IDLE/PLAYING/PAUSED/GAME_OVER) with Enter/P transitions implemented
Correct road lane config: 5 lanes with correct colors, speeds, directions matching spec
Correct river lane config: 5 lanes with log/turtle types, correct colors/speeds/directions
Home bays at correct x positions [20,140,260,380,500] with fill tracking
Timer with 30s countdown and green→yellow→red color transition
Level progression with 1.15× speed multiplier
Forward scoring with maxRowReached tracking
localStorage high score
weaknesses: **CRITICAL BUG in createPlatforms()**: References undefined variable `i` in the isDiver calculation OUTSIDE the for loop — `const isDiver = (laneIdx === 1 && Math.floor(i / 3) % 2 === 0) || (laneIdx === 3 && i % 2 === 0)` is evaluated before the `for (let i = 0; i < count; i++)` loop, causing ReferenceError or using stale closure. This likely crashes platform creation entirely
**Uses e.key instead of e.code** for input: 'ArrowUp'/'w'/'W' via key property — not layout-independent, and checks both lowercase and uppercase letters separately

**River riding uses fractional column**: `frog.col += lane.speed * ... * dt / CELL_SIZE` modifies col as float, but movement snaps frog.col to integers — this creates jittering as frog oscillates between integer columns while being carried

**Collision detection uses computed 'roadRow' formula** that doesn't match lane-to-row mapping: `const roadRow = 5 - (lane.y - 320) / 40` gives wrong row indices, likely causing vehicles in some lanes to never collide with frog

**River collision has similar row mapping issue**: `const riverRow = 10 - (lane.y - 80) / 40` — produces values like 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 but frog rows for river are 2-7, causing platform-frog matching to fail
**Vehicles and platforms are plain colored rectangles** — no visual detail (no wheels, windows, turtle shells, log texture)
**Frog is a plain green rectangle** with small square eyes — no rounded body, no pupils
**No audio** — no Web Audio API usage at all
**No hop animation** — frog.isMoving flag set but never consumed for visual interpolation; frog teleports between grid cells
**Frog death: uses Date.now() for blink** — `Math.floor(Date.now() / 80) % 2` is frame-rate independent but coupling to wall clock creates unpredictable blink patterns
**Home bay check uses row 1** but spec's home bays are at row 0 (y=40) — frog at row 1 is in the river, not at bays. Frog may never reach bays or trigger bay logic from wrong position
**Filled bay shows tiny 10×10 green square** instead of frog icon
**respawnFrog called on bay fill** decrements lives — filling a bay shouldn't cost a life

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: The game has basic movement and rendering, but row-mapping/collision logic is badly broken, a clear createPlatforms bug references i before declaration, and home/river behavior does not reliably match playable Frogger.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Has a runtime error in createPlatforms() referencing undefined variable 'i' outside its loop, collision detection uses mismatched row calculations making road/river interactions unreliable, and respawnFrog is incorrectly called when filling a bay instead of just resetting position.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation with smooth movement, diving turtle logic, and a complete game loop including level progression.

strengths: event.code for ALL input with event.repeat rejection — cleanest input handling, prevents held-key repeat hops
Hop animation with arc: `Math.sin(progress * Math.PI) * 6` adds vertical bounce during hop — nice visual polish beyond spec requirements
Distinct drawFrogBody() with roundRect, legs (darker green rect), white circular eyes with black pupils — reused for bay icons and life display at different scales
Diving turtles: correct 7s cycle with 8Hz blink via Math.floor((cycle-3)*8*2)%2. Correct shouldDive logic: lane 1 every 3rd (itemIndex+1)%3===0, lane 3 every 2nd (itemIndex+1)%2===0
River riding: platform.speed * dt added to frog.x directly — correct continuous carry
Death animation: 0.5s flash with red-colored frog body (deathTimer > 0 triggers red variant of drawFrogBody)
5 audio effects: hop (square 600Hz 30ms), splash (triangle 500→120Hz descending), squash (square 110→60Hz), bay (square 440→880Hz), level (two-stage triangle arpeggio)
Home bays drawn with stroke border (#223388), filled shows scaled frog icon, correct gap detection (no bay overlap = death)
Timer bar shrinks from RIGHT side: `ctx.fillRect(590 - width, 680, width, 12)` — visually distinct shrinking direction
Vehicle visual: dark wheel strips at bottom + translucent white windshield area
Platform logs: dark vertical grain lines every 28px. Turtles: individual ellipses per turtle in group with darker inner rect
Lives display uses scaled frog icons (0.42 scale) — consistent visual language
Clean state machine with separate renderScene() + overlay pattern
updateDivingTurtles runs even during IDLE state — turtles animate on start screen background
weaknesses: **evaluateLanding() has row range gaps**: rows 7-10 return immediately without road collision check on landing — road collisions only checked in the continuous update loop, meaning a frog hopping INTO a vehicle might not die until next frame
**River lane lookup uses getLaneByY(laneY(frog.row))** which requires exact y match — if lane.y values don't exactly equal PLAY_Y + row*CELL, lookup fails silently and frog drowns on valid platforms
**Splash sound uses triangle oscillator 500→120Hz** instead of spec's noise burst — sounds tonal rather than splashy

**Turtle diving: all turtles in diver lanes are marked diver=true** in createMoverLane (diver property set from config.diver for entire lane), but shouldDive check in updateDivingTurtles gates per-index — this is correct functionally but creates confusing dual state where item.diver=true but the turtle doesn't actually dive
**Start screen renders behind scene** — drawStartScreen called after renderScene in IDLE, but renderScene draws game world which is partially visible underneath. Minor visual layering issue

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable Frogger with solid movement, hazards, homes, lives, timer, pause and restart, but diving turtles are partly broken due to checking lane.type instead of item type and home bays reset each level instead of persisting rounds like classic Frogger.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core Frogger mechanics are well-implemented with road traffic, river logs/turtles, diving turtles, home bays, levels, timer, and lives, though the home bay landing detection can be finicky and the row-to-lane mapping has minor alignment issues between visual zones and collision logic.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented Frogger clone with smooth animations, diving turtle logic, and a complete game loop including audio and high scores.

strengths: Runs without JS errors
Basic frog movement and vehicle collision functional
Vehicles wrap around screen edges
weaknesses: **Wrong canvas size**: 600×600 instead of spec's 600×700
**Not Frogger**: No river, no logs, no turtles, no home bays — just vehicles on roads. Missing the core dual-zone mechanic that defines Frogger
**No grid-snapped movement**: frog.speed = 30 (not 40px cell), free-pixel positioning, no grid alignment
**No game states**: No IDLE/PAUSED/GAME_OVER screens, no Enter to start, no P to pause. Game starts immediately
**Uses e.key instead of e.code**: 'ArrowLeft' via key property
**No hop animation**: instant teleport on keypress
**Win condition uses alert()**: `alert('YOU WIN!')` blocks the thread — terrible UX, no proper game over screen
**No lives system**: single death resets frog position, no life counter
**No scoring**: no score variable, no HUD, no high score
**No timer**: no 30s countdown, no timer bar
**No level progression**: single static difficulty
**No audio**: completely silent
**No visual detail**: green rect frog, red rect vehicles, grey lane backgrounds
**No home bays**: reaching top triggers alert win, not bay-fill mechanic
**No localStorage**: no persistence
**Vehicle speeds use Math.random()**: non-deterministic speeds (2 + random*2) rather than spec's defined per-lane speeds
**5 lanes at 80px height**: doesn't match spec's 40px cell grid with 5 road + 5 river lanes
**Simultaneous key handling with priority**: if/else-if chain means only one direction per frame, and 'ArrowLeft' takes priority over 'ArrowUp'

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Basic frog movement and car avoidance work, but this is a very incomplete Frogger clone with wrong canvas size, no river/logs/turtles/home bays, intrusive alert-based win flow, and minimal visuals.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Minimal implementation with only road lanes (no river, logs, turtles, or home bays), wrong canvas size (600x600 vs 600x700), uses alert() for win which blocks the game loop, no lives/score/timer, no game over or restart screen, and visually bears almost no resemblance to Frogger.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: The game is missing the entire river section, logs, turtles, and home bays specified in the rubric, providing only a basic road-crossing mechanic.

rankings.summary: Most faithful to spec. Best grid-based movement with col/row tracking. Correct diveEvery grouping (every 3rd/2nd). Seamless river riding via ridingVx applied during hops. justPressed input pattern with event.code. Complete audio. Clean level recreation. Only minor blink-rate irregularity in dive phases.

Best visual detail (vehicle windows/wheels/headlights, turtle shells with heads, log grain+endcaps). Complete audio suite with 5-note level jingle. Slight hop interpolation overshoot. River riding pauses during hop animation causing micro-drift. Per-platform dive offset creates wave pattern instead of grouped diving.

Cleanest input handling (event.code + event.repeat rejection). Nice hop arc animation. Correct per-index dive logic. But evaluateLanding has row-range gaps, river lane lookup fragile on exact y-match, and splash sound is tonal rather than noise burst.

Critical createPlatforms bug (undefined variable i outside loop). Row mapping formulas wrong for both road and river collision. Frog riding uses fractional column causing jitter. No audio. No hop animation. Bay check at wrong row. respawnFrog on bay fill costs a life. Plain rectangles throughout.

Not Frogger. Road-only with no river/logs/turtles/bays. Wrong canvas size. No grid movement. alert() for win. No states/lives/score/timer/audio/localStorage. Random vehicle speeds. Fourth consecutive last-place finish.

delta_test.notes: GPT-5.4 re-scored o3-mini frogger. Minor upward revision (+0.13) - confirms original QA was calibrated correctly. o3-mini's frogger has basic movement but lacks riding mechanic and timer.
